data.title: China Mengniu Dairy Forecasts Plunge in 2024 Profit; Shares Jump 10%
data.description:
China Mengniu Dairy forecasts its attributable profit to plunge to between 50 million yuan and 250 million yuan in 2024 from 4.81 billion yuan according to a Tuesday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

Revenue is seen to decline during the year due to an imbalance between supply and demand for raw milk, while its Australian subsidiary is seen to incur a loss during the year, the filing said.

China Modern Dairy Holdings, an associated firm, is seen to recognize additional losses from fair value changes less costs. Mengniu's share of losses from the company is slated between 790 million yuan and 900 million yuan, the filing said.

Shares surged 10% during Wednesday's afternoon trading.

data.title: U.K. Inflation Reaches 10-Month High, Complicating Bank of England's Rate Path — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

U.K. annual inflation in January reached its highest level since March last year, complicating the picture for the Bank of England after it said it would cut interest rates gradually amid a weak outlook for economic growth.

Consumer prices were 3.0% higher in January than a year earlier, up from 2.5% in December, the Office for National Statistics said Wednesday. Economists had expected annual inflation to rise to 2.8% according to a poll compiled by The Wall Street Journal.

That meant inflation rose further above the bank's 2% target, suggesting the BOE will find it more difficult to bring interest rates down in the hopes of boosting an economy that grew 0.1% in the final quarter of 2024.

The bank cut its key rate to 4.5% at its latest meeting earlier in February, with Governor Andrew Bailey saying it would take a "gradual and careful" approach to reducing rates ahead. The BOE has cut rates at a slower pace than both the Federal Reserve and the European Central Bank since the summer.

However, the uptick in inflation was expected by BOE policymakers. January's increase in inflation was a result in a jump in air fares, as well as private-school fees, which became subject to value-added tax at the start of the year.

Wage data published Tuesday also showed accelerating pay growth, which will likely knock on to inflation, particularly in the labor-intensive services sector.

Services inflation surged to 5.0% in January, up from 4.4% in December, while core prices that exclude more volatile food and energy were up 3.7%, from 3.2% in the last month of 2024.

The climb in inflation will be uncomfortable for the BOE, though not enough to prevent it from cutting interest rates further, Capital Economics deputy chief U.K. economist Ruth Gregory said in a note to clients.

"But it will mean it continues to cut rates only slowly," she added.

Inflation is expected to rise even further, peaking at 3.7% by the end of summer on the back of higher energy costs, the bank said in its latest macroeconomic forecasts.

They were the same forecasts that slashed economic-growth expectations for 2025--to 0.75% from 1.5% previously--symbolizing the contrasting concerns of stubborn inflation and anemic growth for the bank.

Furthermore, the uncertainty of trade tariffs from the Trump administration threatens to derail attempts to put the economy on a firmer footing. BOE's Bailey said Tuesday that he would prefer less volatility from the U.S., and that fragmentation of trade was bad for global growth.

Trade concerns could also further weaken the pound against the U.S. dollar, which would push prices of energy and other imports higher. The Federal Reserve has also given no suggestion it is in a rush to lower borrowing costs, suggesting investors will stick with the currency offering higher yield returns.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: CARE Affirms AA- Rating on JK Tyre's Long-term Bank Financing; Outlook Stable
data.description:
CARE Ratings affirmed its AA- rating on JK Tyre & Industries' long-term bank facilities and A1+ rating on the company's short-term bank financing.

The rating outlook is stable, according to a Tuesday filing with the Indian bourses.

data.title: NB Private Equity Partners Boosts Capital Reserve for Share Buybacks
data.description:
NB Private Equity Partners boosted its capital reserve for its share buyback program for the next three years.

The investment company reserved $120 million to be available for share buybacks over the next three years, according to a Wednesday release.

The company expects to return $250 million to shareholders over the next three years based on the current dividend level and fully using the additional allocated capital, it said.

data.title: High U.K. Inflation Makes BOE Rate Cut in March Unlikely — Market Talk
data.description: U.K. annual CPI inflation rising more than expected to 3.0% in January from 2.5% in December means it's unlikely that the Bank of England will cut interest rates in March, Abrdn's Luke Bartholomew says in a note. Gradual rate cuts should continue, however, as measures of underlying inflation were more encouraging, with services inflation weaker than expected. "Another rate cut in March looks pretty unlikely, with the Bank [of England] continuing with its gradual pace of easing for now," the deputy chief economist says. Inflation would need to head back towards the 2% target for the BOE to speed up the pace of rate cuts in the second half of the year. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: BYD Faces Backlash in Auto Complaints Forum Following Smart EV Launch
data.description:
BYD's offer to include free smart driving features on its cars was the subject of complaints on a Chinese auto quality platform, Reuters reported Tuesday.

The comments came from customers who said they had overpaid for their vehicles, the report said.

The platform, 12365auto.com, logged more than 4,700 complaints regarding BYD from Feb. 11 to 17, surging from 150 in the previous week and 500 for January, Reuters said.

BYD models also figured in the top 10 of a list ranking the cars based on complaints received, Reuters said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Indonesia Central Bank Holds Rates as It Eyes Rupiah, Trade Risks
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

Indonesia's central bank hit the pause button again, holding rates steady after a surprise cut last month as it monitors currency headwinds and rising external risks.

Bank Indonesia kept its benchmark seven-day reverse repo rate at 5.75% on Wednesday, in a decision analysts had expected to be a close one. Five out of seven economists polled by The Wall Street Journal had forecast a hold, while two had projected a 25-basis-point cut.

The decision is aimed at keeping inflation on target, and supporting growth amid external uncertainties, Bank Indonesia Gov. Perry Warjiyo said at a press conference. The central bank also held its overnight deposit facility rate at 5.00% and its lending facility rate at 6.50%.

The move comes as global central banks express concerns about the unpredictability of policymaking under the Trump administration, and the risks posed to growth and trade. Indonesia, Southeast Asia's largest economy, is exposed to commodity price shocks, but looks more insulated against tariff risks than others in Asia thanks to its relatively smaller trade surplus with the U.S. and domestic-driven growth, analysts say.

Bank Indonesia's main concern is likely the rupiah, which has come under pressure amid trade uncertainty and dollar strength, depreciating about 1.4% versus the greenback year to date, according to LSEG data.

BI Gov. Warjiyo said Wednesday that the currency remains under control despite the rate cut in January, and has been strengthening in February, maintaining relative stability compared to the currencies of Indonesia's key trading partners.

More rate cuts could be in the pipeline, with the central bank having surprised markets in January by lowering rates despite an unsteady rupiah, but it remains to be seen what policymakers will prioritize.

DBS economist Radhika Rao believes Indonesia's monetary policy will need to do more of the heavy lifting in supporting growth this year, as the government's focus remains on fiscal consolidation amid efforts to raise revenue collections. That effort is in question after the government deferred a VAT rate increase and introduced stimulus measures to spur growth, part of which is being financed via a rationalization in spending, she said.

Indonesia's government has announced a multibillion-dollar stimulus package for 2025 as it looks to hit an ambitious 8% economic growth target.

Increased government spending could help boost household consumption, supporting economic growth, CIMB economists said in a recent note. Coupled with external uncertainties, signs of better growth could keep Bank Indonesia in wait-and-see mode, strategically reducing the policy rate only when a favorable window opens, they said.

If the currency remains stable, there's potential for further easing in the second quarter, Barclays economist Brian Tan said in a recent note. A wider trade surplus in January could also strengthen BI's confidence in managing external pressures and supports its growth-focused stance, he added.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

Beeks’ H1 FY25 Profit Jumps 31%, “Trading in Line with Expectations”
data.description:
Beeks Financial Cloud Group (AIM: BKS) released a trading update today (Wednesday), expecting to close the first six months of the fiscal year 2025 with £15.8 million in revenue and £1.8 million in pre-tax profits. The company's accounting period runs from July to June.

A Strong Year for Beeks

The expected six-month revenue for the company, a cloud computing and connectivity provider for financial markets, jumped by 22 percent from the corresponding period of the previous fiscal year, while profits increased by 31 percent.

The trading update further highlighted that the numbers for H1 FY25 are in line with the board's expectations.Gordon McArthur, CEO of Beeks

“We have continued to deliver strong growth in the first half and prove our ability to secure deals with the largest exchanges globally,” said Gordon McArthur, CEO of Beeks.

These figures came after the London-based company closed FY24 with revenue 27 percent higher than the previous year.

Furthermore, the company achieved a positive free cash flow position after working capital movements. At the end of December 2024, its unaudited net cash stood at £6.6 million, the same as at the end of FY24. It also received a delayed post-payment of £1.2 million in early January.

Demand for Cloud Services Remains High

Beeks also pointed out that the strong momentum for its Exchange Cloud service continued. It secured a further extension to the Johannesburg Stock Exchange contract and received approval for a contract with “one of the largest exchanges globally.” Further, after the accounting period, it secured a new deal with Grupo Bolsa Mexicana de Valores, the second-largest exchange in Latin America.

“With a robust business model and an established reputation as a technology provider to financial markets, we are confident in converting our strong pipeline of additional Exchange Cloud opportunities and achieving a full-year financial performance in line with the board’s expectations,” McArthur added.

data.title: Radium Development Seeks Shareholder Nod to Renew Buyback Authority
data.description:
Radium Development is seeking its shareholders' approval to renew share buyback authority to purchase up to 10% of the issued shares of the company.

The property developer will table a resolution to renew the share buy-back authority in the company's upcoming annual general meeting, according to a Tuesday Malaysian bourse disclosure.

Radium Development will share a statement containing details of the repurchase plan with shareholders in due time, it noted.

data.title: Bayer Receives FDA Endorsement to Accelerate Development of Parkinson Treatment
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

Bayer's subsidiary AskBio received approval from the U.S. drug regulator to accelerate the development of its investigational gene therapy to treat moderate-stage Parkinson's disease.

The German pharmaceutical company said Wednesday that its treatment received advanced therapy designation from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration after its early-stage study showed slowed disease progression and improved motor outcomes in patients after 36 months.

The study demonstrated a favorable safety profile and the experimental drug was well tolerated by patients, the company said.

Bayer is currently recruiting patients for its mid-stage study of the experimental drug in the U.S.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: Carnival Unit Prices $1 Billion Offering of Senior Unsecured Notes
data.description:
Cruise operator Carnival said Wednesday its Carnival Corp unit priced its private offering of $1 billion 5.750% senior unsecured notes due March 15, 2030.

Proceeds will be used to redeem the cruise operator's $1 billion 10.500% senior unsecured notes due 2030 as part of the group's strategy to lower interest expense.

Closing of the offering is expected Feb. 28, subject to customary requirements.

data.title: Singapore Budget Should Benefit Grocers, Malls — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's grocers and malls are set to be the biggest beneficiaries of generous handouts from the country's budget, say DBS Group Research analysts in a note. A set of support measures was announced to help households and individuals tackle rising cost-of-living pressures. Various vouchers will support retail-related spending, particularly on essentials at suburban retail malls, DBS says. Among other beneficiaries are supermarkets and food-court operators, the bank adds. Sheng Siong Group is best positioned to gain as a pure domestic grocery retailer, DBS says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: International Public Partnerships Plans to Extend, Expand GBP60 Million Share Buyback Program
data.description:
International Public Partnerships plans to expand and extend its ongoing share buyback program of up to 60 million pounds sterling if its shares continue to trade at a significant discount to net asset value.

The infrastructure investment company repurchased 53 million pounds of shares under the program, which runs until the end of the first quarter.

data.description:

  • However, that did not materialise

data.title: Market Chatter: Shein Assures Investors After US Ends Duty-Free Treatment on Low-Cost Parcels
data.description:
Shein executive chairman Donald Tang reassured investors that "growth remains strong" even if the US will end duty-free treatment of low-cost parcels from China following tariffs against the country, Reuters reported Tuesday.

Growth is "driven by our ability to offer a diverse selection of fashion and lifestyle products at consistently affordable prices," Reuters quoted Tang as saying.

Shein is enhancing its supply chain "to boost efficiency and responsiveness" to ensure faster deliveries, the report said, citing Tang's note.

The China-founded fast-fashion company is on its way to pursuing an IPO in London but the tariff exemptions on parcels ended by US President Donald Trump sparked concerns about Shein's model, the report said.

The development could trigger Shein and Temu, owned by Pinduoduo, to increase prices, Reuters said, citing analysts.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Market Chatter: China New Home Prices Barely Fall; Analysts 'Less Bearish' on Housing Sector
data.description:
New home prices in China barely moved downward in January for the fifth straight month, signaling the start of stability in pricing, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday.

Prices of new homes in 70 cities declined 0.07% in January, slower than the 0.08% fall seen in December 2024, according to data from the National Bureau of Statistics.

Second-hand home prices declined 0.34% in January compared with 0.31% in December, the report said.

The narrower decline could be seen as a sign of hope for the struggling property sector, the report said.

However, China will need a solid rebound in prices to put a floor to declining prices, Bloomberg reported, citing a note by Fitch Ratings.

Other analysts are turning "less bearish on the sector" from February with better-than-expected home sales from the beginning of January and an increase in homebuyer visits, the report said, citing analysts at Citigroup.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals' FY24 Gold, Silver Production Exceeds Guidance Range
data.description:
Wheaton Precious Metals said Wednesday its gold and silver production for 2024 rose year over year and exceeded its guidance range.

Gold production totaled 379,742 ounces, compared with 374,585 ounces a year ago. Silver production came in at 20.7 million ounces, compared with 17.2 million ounces in 2023.

Wheaton's 2024 production guidance for gold was between 325,000 ounces and 370,000 ounces and the silver production guidance was between 18.5 million ounces and 20.5 million ounces.

For 2025, the Canadian precious metals streaming company expects gold production of between 350,000 ounces and 390,000 ounces, and silver production is expected in the range of 20.5 million ounces to 22.5 million ounces.

Wheaton is also targeting a 40% increase in production to 870,000 gold equivalent ounces by 2029.

data.title: Hainan Haiyao Subsidiary Gains Approval for Fengliao Stomach Health Tablet License
data.description:
Hainan Haiyao wholly-owned subsidiary, Haikou Pharmaceutical Factory, received approval from China's National Medical Products Administration to become the marketing authorization holder for Fengliao Stomach Health Dispersible Tablets, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shenzhen Bourse.

The drug, used for digestive issues like acute gastroenteritis, remains unchanged in the formula and production site. This approval strengthens the company's product lineup and market competitiveness.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
Stocks Edge Higher; Intel Shares Rally

U.S. stocks rose slightly to start the week. Intel shares jumped after The Wall Street Journal reported that Broadcom and TSMC were eyeing separate deals that would break the U.S. chip maker in two.

Trump Floats 25% Tariffs on Autos, Chips, Pharmaceuticals

The president provided further details about his expected tariff moves.

U.K. Inflation Reaches 10-Month High

Consumer prices were 3.0% higher in January than a year earlier, up from 2.5% in December, complicating the picture for the BOE after it said it would cut interest rates gradually.

China Wants Its Economy to Grow 5%. Consumers Aren't Buying It.

Chinese consumers are nervous about the political and economic environments. Their pocketbooks are shut.

China Home Prices Show Slight Improvement But Remain in Decline

Home prices in major Chinese cities continued to slightly narrow their declines, suggesting that Beijing's policy blitz has helped stabilize the beleaguered real-estate sector.

BOJ's Takata Calls for Gradual Rate Hikes, Sees Upside Risks to Prices

The Bank of Japan needs to keep adjusting the degree of monetary accommodation in stages, policy board member Hajime Takata said as he warned about inflation risks.

RBNZ Slashes Rates Further, Sounds Global Trade Warning

The Reserve Bank of New Zealand slashed interest rates by a further 50 basis points on Wednesday, continuing its campaign to restart a stalled economy.

Fed Minutes Should Give More Clues About Rate Cut Timing

Minutes from the Federal Open Market Committee's Jan. 28-29 meeting will be released Wednesday afternoon at 2 p.m. Eastern time.

Howard Lutnick Is Confirmed as Commerce Secretary With Tariff Fights Heating Up

The billionaire Wall Street veteran is joining an economic team that includes a trade hawk, Peter Navarro.

U.S., Russia Forge New Path on Ukraine After First High-Level Talks

The announcement of a formal process to try to halt the Ukraine war followed talks in which Washington attempted to reset its tense relationship with Moscow.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
HSBC Posts Quarterly Net Profit, Plans $2 Billion Share Buyback

The London-based bank booked net profit of $197 million for the three months ended December, beating estimates by Visible Alpha.

Glencore Earnings Fall; Plans $2.2 Billion in Shareholder Returns

Glencore's earnings declined on lower coal prices, missing market views, but the mining and trading giant still plans to return around $2.2 billion to shareholders.

Apple's Big Launch Could Bring a Cheaper iPhone

CEO Tim Cook promised a new product today, and Wall Street is expecting a new version of its iPhone SE, which could boost sales in China.

Shift4 Strikes $1.5 Billion Deal for Tax-Free Shopping Business

The expected $7.50-per-share offer for Global Blue Group represents a 15% premium to where the tax-free shopping services provider's shares closed last week.

Dell expected to provide lower guidance next week, but AI servers remain a big bright spot

Here's why one analyst advises, "Don't bet against Michael Dell."

Bumble Shares Tumble on Weak Outlook, Rebound Efforts Continue

The online-dating company posted operating earnings of $37 million, compared with an operating loss of $6.9 million a year earlier.

Activist Launches Proxy Fight at National Health Investors

Land & Buildings argues the REIT is too closely tied to a large tenant and could get better lease terms.

Toll Brothers Misses Earnings Estimates. The Stock Falls.

Earnings that came in below estimates were due to a delay in the sale of a stabilized apartment property, Toll Brothers said.

Ackman Boosts Bid for Howard Hughes, Vowing to Create Modern Version of Berkshire Hathaway

The revised proposal would increase Pershing Square's stake to 48%.

Air Canada Expects Several Days Before Normalized Service Following Storms, Toronto Crash

The airline said it is restoring service and adding extra flights and capacity where possible but that the reduction in the number of take-offs and landings permitted at its global hub in Toronto is slowing its recovery.

data.title: Telefonica Plans to Raise Up to $2.82 Billion Through Divestments, Expansion Says
data.description:
• Telefonica plans to raise between 2.50 billion euros and 2.70 billion euros ($2.61 billion and $2.82 billion) through different asset divestment processes currently underway in Europe and Latin America, Spanish newspaper Expansion reports, without citing sources.
  • The transactions located in Argentina and Colombia would involve the sale of all subsidiaries based in each of these countries, Expansion says.
  • The group is also considering the sale of minority stakes in fiber or fixed cable telecommunications networks in the U.K. and Spain, the newspaper adds.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/52m24unz

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com
